title: Comms datalink encryption upgrade: AES-256-GCM via P-1127 FPGA, radio P-1125→P-1126
text:
Engineering Change Request ECR-229.

Raised by: Priya Nair (Comms/Datalink Engineer), 2025-01-29.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Felix Herrera (Systems Integration), 2025-02-05.

Summary: Add AES-256-GCM hardware encryption module (P-1127 FPGA) to the datalink, and upgrade the radio from P-1125 (FreeWave 500kbps) to P-1126 (FreeWave 2Mbps dual-band) to accommodate the bandwidth overhead.

Rationale: A penetration test conducted in December 2024 (report MD-SEC-2024-11) found that the K-200 command and telemetry link was vulnerable to command injection — an attacker with a compatible FreeWave radio could inject spoofed command packets. The customer's security addendum (contract KES-203) requires AES-256-GCM end-to-end encryption on all command and telemetry traffic by programme milestone M4 (March 2025). This was agreed at WIKI-071 (Comms Encryption Upgrade Review, 2025-01-22).

Architecture (from WIKI-071): The P-1127 FPGA module (Lattice iCE40, implementing AES-256-GCM in hardware) sits between the datalink controller (P-1121) and the radio (P-1125/P-1126). Session keys are derived via ECDH on each power cycle, with key rotation every 60 minutes. The AES-256-GCM 16-byte auth tag and protocol overhead add approximately 3% throughput overhead. At 500kbps (P-1125) this is marginal; the P-1126 at 2Mbps provides sufficient headroom for full telemetry at 200ms update rate.

Impact:
- P-1125: Obsoleted, replaced by P-1126
- P-1127: New part (FPGA encryption module), added to BOM
- P-1121: Datalink controller board — compatible SPI interface confirmed
- SRC-018 (encryption_handler.c): Implementation complete, unit tests passing
- SRC-024 (gcs_ui_backend.py): Protocol stack update required for encrypted sessions — Jordan Kim
- DOC-471: Comms System ICD to Rev C (new encryption framing)
- DOC-472: Comms Protocol Specification to Rev B
- DRW-531: Enhanced antenna mount drawing to be updated for P-1126 dimensions
- P-1167: GCS Radio Unit — must be swapped to FreeWave GXM unit paired to P-1126
- GCS antenna mounts (P-1165, P-1166): No change
